---
title: "Quantitative Methods in Tourism"
subtitle: "Master in Tourism and Sustainable Development · TIDES · ULPGC"
date: last-modified
---

## Instructors

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Christian González Martel
Modules I & II · *Introduction to R* · *Visual exploratory analysis*

- <christian.gonzalez@ulpgc.es>
- Office **D-4-07**
- Tutorials · Monday 11:00–13:00 · Tuesday 11:00–12:30 · Friday 9:30–12:00
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Juan M. Hernández Guerra
Module III · *Introduction to machine learning*

- <juan.hernandez@ulpgc.es>
- Office **D-4-20**
- Tutorials · Tuesday 9:00–13:00 · Wednesday 9:00–11:00
:::

:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Please email us to make an appointment before coming to tutorials.
:::

## Contents

- **Module I · Introduction to R**
  - Data in R
  - Data wrangling
  - Basic statistics with R
- **Module II · Visual exploratory analysis**
  - One-variable visualisation
  - Multiple-variables visualisation
  - Editing reproducible documents
- **Module III · Introduction to machine learning**
  - Regression models
  - Classification models
  - Text classification (NLP)

## Practical information

|  |  |
|---|---|
| **Classroom** | EA 0.1 |
| **Official exam dates** | June 8 · June 29, 2026 |
| **Campus Virtual** | News, slides, exercises and final exam material |
| **Course repo** | <https://github.com/chrglez/quantitative-methods-master-tides> |
| **Licence** | CC BY-SA 4.0 |

## Schedule · Modules I & II (30 h over 6 days of 5 h)

| Day | Morning | Afternoon |
|:---:|---|---|
| 1 | Git & GitHub · Data in R | Guided exercise |
| 2 | Data wrangling (tidyverse) | Guided exercise |
| 3 | Basic statistics with R | Graded exercise |
| 4 | One-variable visualisation | Graded exercise |
| 5 | Multi-variable visualisation | Graded exercise |
| 6 | Editing reproducible documents | Final submission |

# Evaluation system {background-color="#0067a2"}

## Weights

::: {.incremental}
- **In-class exercises · 50 %**
  - Guided exercises · **25 %**
  - Proposed exercises · **25 %**
- **Final exercise · 50 %**
- **Pass threshold · 50 %** overall
:::

## Weighting across modules

::: {.callout-important}
Modules **I and II combined** count as **50 %** of the subject mark.
Module **III** accounts for the other **50 %**.
:::

So within the first half of the subject, each module weighs the same:
Module I contributes 25 % and Module II contributes 25 % of the final
subject mark.

## How you submit {.smaller}

Every in-class and final exercise is handed in via your personal fork
of the course repository on GitHub:

1. **Fork** <https://github.com/chrglez/quantitative-methods-master-tides>.
2. **Clone** your fork, open the `.qmd` template under `exercises/dayN/`,
   fill it in.
3. **Commit and push** to your fork.
4. Paste the commit URL in the submission form on Campus Virtual.

The full Git workflow is covered in the first hour of Day 1.

## Resources

- **Slides** · <https://chrglez.github.io/quantitative-methods-master-tides/>
- **Campus Virtual** · all announcements, exercise briefs and grades.
- **Office hours** · see top slide — email first.

# Let's get started {background-color="#0067a2"}

Welcome aboard.
